title: Add --dangling option to rmi command
body:
Hi,

After using buildah for a while I ended up with many dangling images at my images list, as described at issue #410. Checking the comments on such issue it was suggested adding a --dangling option to rmi. This is my try to implement such a feature. If you think this is not the correct approach, I am happy to re-work this patch. 

Thanks

comments.body:
Can one of the admins verify this patch?
I understand the following commands:
- `bot, add author to whitelist`
- `bot, test pull request`
- `bot, test pull request once`
bot, add author to whitelist
@artmello Make your code into a function removeDangling() and call it, that should fix the complexity issue.
Thanks everyone for reviewing. I changed the code so hopefully complexity under rmiCmd() is reduced and commit should be signed. Still missing some new tests for it, that I will be working next.
Can you add a test.
New test added
@rh-atomic-bot r+

:pushpin: Commit 967a045 has been approved by `rhatdan`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=rhatdan 967a0452f4001461eefdf32b21706879ae346bf8 -->
:hourglass: Testing commit 967a045 with merge 831fdef...

The changes LGTM, but after to testing/thinking about this, I'd like to change the name of the parameter from "dangling" to "prune".  Two reasons.  I'm not at  all happy with the short version -d as it's generally used for delete.  Secondly the functionality that does this in Docker is 'docker system prune'.  

@rhatdan WDYT?  I see this is close to being merged.  I'm happy to change it afterwards.

On those lines, would make sense instead of having --dangling option, having a --filter one as we have for "images" command? Would be a little more verbose than --dangling (would be something like --filter "dangling=true"), but you could remove all images that meet some filter and not only dangling ones. WDYT?
:boom: Test timed out

Personally I'd rather the prune than the filter.  Part of that is personal bias, I've never been a big fan of filter options (yet I grep output like crazy), and I'd like to somewhat match the functions that Docker provides.  @rhatdan and @nalind are the folks that set the valid Buildah commands, I'll let them weigh in.
I like prune.
I substitute --dangling,-d option to --prune,-p on rmi command. WDYT?
@TomSweeneyRedHat @rhatdan I saw CI failed. Should I close PR and reopen it?

@artmello really good work on this.  I just have a bunch of minor nits to tend to that will make long term code support more clear.  Thank you!  Plus hopefully a new submit will cleanse the Red Hat CI daemon issues away.  I'm sure it's not related at all to your changes.
Sorry, hit the wrong button, didn't mean to close it.
@TomSweeneyRedHat thanks a lot for your review. I tried to address the comments on this version.
@rh-atomic-bot r+
